skill.name: outlook-automation
skill.description: Automate Microsoft Outlook email, calendar, contact, folder, and attachment workflows through the Rube MCP Outlook toolkit.
skill.summary: Use Rube MCP Outlook tools to search mail, inspect attachments, manage calendars, and organize contacts.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static external command, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance detections are false positives caused by Markdown code formatting and Outlook tool names. The confirmed risk is medium because the skill requires a third-party Rube MCP endpoint and can access sensitive Outlook email, calendar, contact, folder, and attachment data.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Endpoint Handles Outlook Access
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add https://rube.app/mcp and complete Microsoft OAuth through Rube. This is intended functionality, but it routes mailbox and calendar automation through an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint and OAuth workflow are explicit in the setup instructions. The risk is privacy and authorization exposure, not confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Outlook Data Operations
medium_findings.description: The documented workflows include searching messages, downloading attachments, listing events, checking schedules, and managing contacts. These operations can expose sensitive personal or business data if permissions are overbroad.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The tool sequences clearly describe mailbox, attachment, calendar, and contact access. This is legitimate for the skill but needs clear user consent and permission boundaries.

low_findings.title: External Command Detections Are Markdown Tool References
low_findings.description: The many Ruby backtick findings are false positives. The backticks format tool names, parameter names, and filter examples in Markdown; no shell execution code is present in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown documentation, not executable Ruby or shell code. No command interpreter invocation or user-controlled shell string is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detection Is Unsupported
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography flag points to the description line, which only names Outlook automation and Rube MCP. I found no cryptographic algorithm or hashing implementation in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains descriptive metadata only. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other weak cryptography in the file.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detections Are Parameter Documentation
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance flags point to Outlook message identifiers and quick-reference rows. These lines document API parameters and do not collect host, network, process, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines refer to Outlook resource identifiers and tool parameters. I found no commands or instructions for local system discovery.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Network Dependency For Microsoft OAuth
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup directs users to add an external MCP server and complete Microsoft OAuth. Users should verify the service and requested scopes before granting Outlook access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external endpoint and OAuth flow are plainly documented. This is a legitimate integration pattern but creates a meaningful trust boundary.

content.user_title: Automate Outlook Workflows with Rube MCP
content.value_statement: Outlook work often spans email search, calendars, contacts, folders, and attachments. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP workflows for Microsoft 365 accounts.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for the Rube MCP Outlook toolkit and Microsoft OAuth connection.
Searches Outlook messages with KQL across folders when supported by the account.
Queries messages inside specific folders with OData filters and pagination guidance.
Lists and inspects calendar events, calendar views, schedules, and calendars.
Lists, creates, and organizes contacts and contact folders.
Lists and downloads Outlook message attachments through documented tool sequences.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP availability and an active Outlook connection.
Some search features require Microsoft 365 or Enterprise accounts.
Personal Outlook and Hotmail accounts may have limited API access.
Recent emails may not appear immediately because Outlook search indexing can lag.

use_cases.title: Find Time-Sensitive Emails
use_cases.description: Search across Outlook folders for unread, high-priority, or attachment-bearing messages and inspect the relevant results.

use_cases.title: Prepare For Meetings
use_cases.description: List calendar events, review attendee responses, and check free or busy windows before scheduling follow-up work.

use_cases.title: Organize Contacts And Folders
use_cases.description: Create contacts, review contact folders, and organize mail folders using Outlook tool sequences.

prompt_templates.title: Check Outlook Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the outlook-automation skill to confirm Rube MCP is connected. Search for current Outlook tool schemas first, then tell me whether my Outlook connection is active.

prompt_templates.title: Search Recent Messages
prompt_templates.prompt: Find Outlook emails from the last seven days with attachments about invoices. Use the current Rube Outlook schema and summarize the most relevant messages.

prompt_templates.title: Review Calendar Window
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Outlook calendar tools to list my events for next week in America/New_York. Include meeting times, organizer names, and attendee response status when available.

prompt_templates.title: Audit A Mail Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Search all Outlook folders for messages from a customer domain, inspect attachments, identify follow-up meetings, and propose folder or contact updates. Ask before creating anything.

output_examples.input: Find unread high-priority messages in my inbox.
output: Searched the inbox with an unread and high-importance filter.
Returned matching messages with sender, subject, received time, and attachment status.
Flagged that more pages are available when Outlook returns a next link.

output_examples.input: What meetings do I have tomorrow morning?
output: Checked the requested calendar window in the selected timezone.
Listed each event with time, subject, organizer, and response status.
Noted any recurring events that require expanded occurrence review.

output_examples.input: Create a contact for a new customer.
output: Collected the required contact name and email details.
Confirmed the target contact folder before creation.
Reported the created contact details after the Outlook tool completed.

best_practices: Search Rube tools first so requests use the current Outlook schemas.
Confirm OAuth connection status and requested scopes before accessing mailbox data.
Ask before creating contacts, folders, or downloading attachments.
anti_patterns: Do not assume personal Outlook accounts support every Microsoft 365 search feature.
Do not use email fields in calendar filters or calendar fields in email filters.
Do not process attachments unless the user confirms the target message and file.

faq.question: Does this skill send data to a third-party service?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses the Rube MCP endpoint for Outlook automation, so users should review the service and OAuth scopes.

faq.question: Can it search all Outlook folders?
faq.answer: It can guide cross-folder search with OUTLOOK_SEARCH_MESSAGES when the connected account supports that feature.

faq.question: Can it work with Hotmail or personal Outlook accounts?
faq.answer: Some features may be limited. The skill notes that Microsoft 365 or Enterprise accounts support more search capabilities.

faq.question: Can it download attachments?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents attachment listing and download tool sequences, but users should confirm each file before download.

faq.question: Can it create contacts or folders?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents contact creation, contact folders, and mail folder creation through Outlook tools.

faq.question: Does it include executable scripts?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed file is Markdown guidance for Rube MCP tools and does not include executable code.
